questions.q: Which Indian leader most famously promoted khadi?
questions.explanation: Gandhi promoted khadi as a symbol of self-reliance and resistance to colonial textiles.

questions.q: Khadi is best described as a fabric that is how made?
questions.explanation: Khadi is cloth that is both handspun and handwoven by hand.

questions.q: Khadi became a symbol of resistance against the import of which British product?
questions.explanation: Khadi resisted British textile imports that had undermined Indian handlooms.

questions.q: Wearing khadi during the freedom struggle was seen as a badge of what?
questions.explanation: Wearing khadi marked a person as part of the national movement.

questions.q: Which simple device is used to make the yarn for khadi?
questions.explanation: Yarn for khadi is spun on the charkha, the hand-operated spinning wheel.

questions.q: Khadi was promoted as a means of economic what?
questions.explanation: Khadi embodied economic self-reliance, or swadeshi.

questions.q: Khadi production was meant especially to help which section of India?
questions.explanation: Khadi gave supplementary income and dignity to India's rural poor.

questions.q: Which natural fibre is most commonly used to make khadi?
questions.explanation: Khadi is most commonly made from handspun cotton, though silk and wool khadi also exist.

questions.q: Promoting khadi was closely tied to boycotting which kind of cloth?
questions.explanation: The khadi campaign went hand in hand with boycotting foreign mill-made cloth.

questions.q: Khadi is most strongly associated with which broader campaign for Indian-made goods?
questions.explanation: Khadi was central to the Swadeshi ideal of using Indian-made goods.

questions.q: Which organization did Gandhi help establish in 1925 to promote khadi production?
options: The Indian National Trade Union Congress
questions.explanation: The All India Spinners' Association (Akhil Bharat Charkha Sangh) was founded in 1925 to promote khadi.

questions.q: During which 1920s movement did the burning of foreign cloth and adoption of khadi become widespread?
questions.explanation: Khadi adoption surged during the Non-Cooperation Movement of 1920-22, with bonfires of foreign cloth.

questions.q: Khadi was promoted as a revival of which earlier movement's call for indigenous goods?
questions.explanation: Khadi drew on the Swadeshi spirit first roused by the 1905 partition of Bengal.

questions.q: Gandhi often described the act of spinning khadi yarn as a form of what?
options: A daily discipline and constructive work
questions.explanation: Gandhi saw spinning as constructive work and a daily discipline of self-reliance.

questions.q: For many Congress workers, regular spinning of khadi yarn became a what?
options: A required form of party membership contribution
questions.explanation: Congress at times required members to spin and contribute yarn as a sign of commitment.

questions.q: Khadi clothing became a visible uniform of which political organization?
questions.explanation: White khadi became the recognizable dress of Congress workers and leaders.

questions.q: Khadi was intended to address rural unemployment by providing what?
options: Supplementary income from spinning and weaving
questions.explanation: Khadi gave villagers extra income through spinning and weaving in their spare time.

questions.q: Which colour of khadi became iconic as the attire of nationalist leaders?
questions.explanation: Plain white khadi became the iconic dress of the freedom movement's leaders.

questions.q: Khadi was promoted as part of Gandhi's wider programme known as what?
questions.explanation: Khadi was a key element of Gandhi's Constructive Programme for nation-building.

questions.q: Khadi resistance struck at the profits of textile mills based mainly in which country?
questions.explanation: Khadi was a direct economic blow to British textile mills, especially in Lancashire.

questions.q: Which English county's textile industry was notably hurt by India's khadi boycott of foreign cloth?
questions.explanation: The Lancashire cotton industry was significantly affected by the Indian boycott of foreign cloth.

questions.q: Khadi and the spinning programme aimed to make villages more economically what?
questions.explanation: Khadi aimed at making Indian villages economically self-sufficient.

questions.q: Which body succeeded earlier organizations to oversee khadi after independence?
options: The Khadi and Village Industries Commission
questions.explanation: The Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) was set up in 1956 to promote khadi and village industries.

questions.q: In which year was the All India Spinners' Association founded to coordinate khadi work?
questions.explanation: The All India Spinners' Association was founded in 1925.

questions.q: Gandhi argued that khadi linked the educated classes with which group through shared labour?
questions.explanation: Gandhi saw khadi as a bond uniting the educated with the poorest masses through manual work.

questions.q: Modern rules generally reserve the term 'khadi' for cloth where both spinning and weaving are done how?
questions.explanation: Genuine khadi requires both hand-spinning and hand-weaving, distinguishing it from mill cloth.

questions.q: Khadi differs from ordinary handloom cloth chiefly because its yarn must also be what?
questions.explanation: Unlike general handloom, khadi requires the yarn itself to be hand-spun, not just hand-woven.

questions.q: Gandhi viewed the promotion of khadi partly as a remedy for the decline of which traditional industry under colonialism?
options: India's handloom and cottage textile industry
questions.explanation: Khadi sought to revive India's handloom and cottage textile industry ruined under colonial policy.

questions.q: Which early national flag design carried a symbol directly tied to the khadi and spinning ideal?
options: The flag bearing the charkha (spinning wheel)
A flag with a crescent only
questions.explanation: The pre-1947 Congress flag featured the charkha, the spinning wheel behind khadi.

questions.q: Khadi's emphasis on decentralized production reflected Gandhi's preference for what kind of economy?
questions.explanation: Khadi expressed Gandhi's vision of a decentralized, village-centred economy over large industry.
